behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical; keys from different parameters never collide
behaviors.justification: Key generation is a pure, deterministic function with no I/O or external dependencies. A mock of storage wouldn't hide failures—the failure is in the hashing/serialization logic itself. Testing the function directly with various parameter combinations (identical, different, edge cases like empty params, special characters) is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that generates cache keys from multiple query parameter sets and verifies: (1) identical parameters always produce identical keys, (2) different parameters produce different keys, (3) no collisions across a large sample space, (4) edge cases like null/empty params, Unicode, ordering variations.

behaviors.description: TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule
behaviors.justification: While TTL calculation itself could be unit-tested with a provided rule object, the described behavior requires testing the full lookup pipeline: load freshness rules from config storage → look up the rule for a given category → compute TTL. A mock of config lookup would hide failures in rule loading, parsing, or category matching. Real config storage (even an in-memory test database) is needed to catch misconfigured rules or lookup failures.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that (1) loads freshness rules from a test config source (in-memory DB or fixture), (2) requests TTL for various data categories, (3) verifies correct TTL values per category (e.g., user_profile=5min, product_catalog=1hr), (4) handles edge cases like undefined categories, invalid rules, and rule updates.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU
behaviors.justification: Testing strategy selection logic alone (Unit) would only catch bugs in the if-statement; it wouldn't catch failures in actual memory measurement or the effectiveness of strategy switching. Mocking memory measurement hides whether real cache operations trigger memory correctly. The real failure is: 'cache fills, memory exceeds threshold, but strategy doesn't switch.' Catching this requires filling a real cache with actual entries, measuring real memory consumption, and verifying strategy behavior changes.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that (1) creates a cache instance with low memory threshold, (2) adds entries until memory usage exceeds threshold, (3) monitors actual memory consumption, (4) verifies the active eviction strategy has switched from LRU to LFU, (5) optionally reverifies that subsequent evictions follow the LFU policy (not LRU).

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows
behaviors.justification: Mocking database writes is the primary failure mode—if the DB write is mocked, the invalidation trigger never fires, and the test always passes even if invalidation is completely broken. The real failure requires a real PostgreSQL instance to test whether writes actually trigger cache invalidation. An in-memory mock cannot replicate row-change events or database hooks. Testing with a real (containerized or test) PostgreSQL database is essential.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that (1) sets up a real PostgreSQL database with a test table, (2) inserts a row and caches the result, (3) performs a write/update/delete on that row in the database, (4) verifies the corresponding cache entry is invalidated, (5) verifies unrelated cache entries are NOT invalidated, (6) tests batch operations and edge cases (multiple rows, no-match writes).

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full
behaviors.justification: Testing only the eviction selection logic (Unit) misses failures in the data structures that track access time or frequency. Mocking access-time or frequency tracking hides real bugs in those mechanisms. The failure—'wrong entry is evicted'—could stem from incorrect tracking, not just wrong selection logic. Testing requires a real cache implementation with actual LRU/LFU tracking structures, filling it to capacity, creating a clear eviction order (via real access patterns), and verifying the correct entry is removed.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that (1) creates a real cache instance with LRU policy, (2) fills it to capacity with tracked entries, (3) establishes a clear LRU order through real access patterns, (4) triggers eviction and verifies the least-recently-used entry is removed, (5) repeats with LFU policy and verifies least-frequently-used is removed, (6) tests edge cases (single entry, all equally old/frequent, multiple evictions).
